name: Mara
manager_notes: Mara is technically excellent and owns our reliability standards, but she keeps getting pulled into every major incident and I worry the strategic platform work she wants is always losing to firefighting. I want to understand what growth direction she actually wants and agree on one concrete step to protect time for it.
answers: Honestly the last month has been heavy on incidents, three sev-1s, so most of my platform work stalled.
I want to make sure we talk about the error budget policy, the feature teams keep treating it as optional.
The on-call rotation is sustainable for me personally but the escalation path still routes too much to me directly.
What energises me is the prevention work, designing the systems so the same outage cannot happen twice.
I would like to grow toward setting reliability direction across teams, not just being the strongest responder.
The blocker is that incident response always wins, there is no protected time for the standards work.
If I could hand the postmortem follow-ups to the service teams with clear owners, that would free real time.
A fair next step would be agreeing which two services adopt the new SLO template this quarter.
Yes, if we lock the SLO rollout as my growth focus and you back me with the team leads, I am committed.
